error.signature: Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find module '/app/utils' imported from /app/index.js

verdict.summary: ESM import cannot find the module - missing file extension in import path.

dead_ends.why_fails: Switches the entire project back to CJS; breaks ES imports
Mixing module systems causes more problems

workarounds.action: Add the .js file extension to imports: import { fn } from './utils.js'
workarounds.how: ESM requires explicit file extensions unlike CJS

workarounds.action: Configure TypeScript or bundler to handle extension rewriting
workarounds.how: In tsconfig: moduleResolution: 'nodenext' adds extensions in output

frequently_confused_with.distinction: 'Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]' is a esm error while 'TypeError [ERR_IMPORT_ASSERTION_TYPE_MISSING]' has a different failure mode despite similar symptoms. Check error message details to differentiate.
